checks.name: A1a sine formula: A v_k == lam_k v_k, lam_k = 4 sin^2(k pi h/2)/h^2
A1b eigh(A) reproduces the closed-form eigenvalues
A1c normalized sines are orthonormal: (2h)^{1/2} V has V^T V = I
A2a heat semigroup: mode k has half-life ln2/lam_k (k=1 and k=64, via expm)
A2b half-life ratio t_half(1)/t_half(64) == kappa; mode 64 is numerically dead (< 1e-12; exact value exp(-kappa ln 2) underflows) at mode 1's half-life
A3a DST-I with scale 1/sqrt(2(n+1)) == projection onto normalized sine modes
A3b GD per-mode contraction measured == (1 - alpha lam_k)^m, all 64 modes, 400 iterations
A3c mode pairing: |1-alpha lam_k| == |1-alpha lam_{65-k}| for every k (lam_k + lam_{65-k} = 4/h^2); slowest pair {1,64} at the rate, fastest pair {32,33}
B1 V^T A V is diagonal (energy = 64 independent parabolas)
B2 the diagonal is lam_k (per-parabola stiffness)
B3 one-pass DST-I solve (transform, divide by lam_k, transform back) == spsolve
C1 deflated spectrum == {1 (x p)} U {alpha_p lam_k, k>p} for all six p
C2 every M_p^{-1} is already optimally damped: p eigenvalues pinned at 1, at least p for each p>0
C3 kappa_eff measured == lam_n/lam_{p+1} for all p
C4 the (p+1)^2 payoff: kappa_eff/(kappa/(p+1)^2) in [1.0, 1.06] for p <= 16 (sublinearity of sin)
C5 measured GD tail slope == log((kappa_eff-1)/(kappa_eff+1)) for all p (rel err)
C6 iterations to 1e-10 drop monotonically with p: 18224 > 4559 > 2028 > 733 > 229 > 67
D1 plain SGD reaches 2x its noise floor in 4142 iterations; with the p=4 head solved exactly, 74 -- transient collapses 56x
D2 measured stationary mean-square error within [0.6, 1.6]x the exact per-mode formula, both runs
D3 the price of the exact head: its noise floor is higher (0.1053 vs 0.0044) -- Newton amplifies noise in flat directions; head-mode stationary variance sigma^2/lam_k^2
E1 none: kappa_eff == kappa == lam_n/lam_1
E2 spectral deflation ledger iterations match section C runs (p=4, p=8)
E3a coarse-average space vs true bottom-p eigenspace: largest principal angle 36.48 deg (p=4), 41.21 deg (p=8) -- cheap imitations, never orthogonal (< 45 deg)
E3b coarse deflation sits between spectral and none: 733 < 1640 < 18224 (p=4, same for p=8)
E4a Nystrom eigenvalue estimates undershoot the TRUE TOP eigenvalues (Lemma 2.1) -- it deflates the top, the harmless end
E4b Nystrom leaves the BOTTOM wrong: kappa_eff(p=8) still > 0.9 kappa, iterations 18156 vs 18224 plain -- barely improves
E4c Nystrom's wrong directions include the lowest mode k=1 (Rayleigh diagnostic), unlike spectral deflation whose k=1..p are exact
E5a block-Jacobi(2): exactly 2 eigenvalues of M^{-1}A differ from 1 (the rank-2 symmetric interface coupling of the single separator bond)
E5b the outliers are exactly 1 +/- 32/33, so kappa_eff = 65 = n+1
E5c clustering vs range (report 13): CG pays per CLUSTER -- PCG in 3 iterations (<= 4); Richardson pays for the RANGE -- 663 iterations (>= 500) at rate 32/33
E6a IC(0) is EXACT on the chain: L L^T == A on the tridiagonal pattern (zero fill-in on a tree/path)
E6b IC(0): zero directions left wrong, Richardson converges in 1 iteration

sgd.notes: Quadratic caricature only: constant-step GD with additive isotropic gradient noise on the chain quadratic. It shows why curvature-aware preconditioning shortens the low-curvature transient in stochastic optimization, and that the exact head solve trades a higher noise floor for a shorter transient. No broader ML claims.
